items.title: Golden Point Print
items.description: My client wanted a Website to Showcase the services provided by his Printing Shop  I created the website using WordPress. Moreover, he wanted to showcase his products with image sliders. Therefore, the task also involved making attractive banners with images provided by the owner and via stock images available on the internet. So, I created a page for each service with JavaScript image sliders in it. I also put his Contact Details on the left Sidebar of each page.

items.title: Fit Fugitives
items.description:
My Client wanted the following customizations to his opencart website:

1)Shopping Flow is as follows

a)  Fugitive Loyalty Member(20%+ off Products)
b)  One Time Retail Purchase
based on the type of checkout the customer chooses the prices would then adjust in the cart to checkout.  Go to http://getfit88.myitworks.

items.title: Complete Controller CRM System
items.description:
My job was to fix some bugs, modify some features and to add some new modules/features into CRM System. Following is the list of instructions that were given to me by the client:

1. Give names to the icons in the upper right of the main admin section (there are three icons there)

2. When we try to add a new company to our company list it prompts for Creator ID (can we make that auto populate based on who is logged in?)

3. We need a ‘Create New Client’ button on the home screen that produces the 5 forms with the next Company ID in succession

4. change Internal ID to Username on all the forms. 

5. Please fix the search function (it doesn't work)

6. Please fix the horizontal scrolling in lists.

7. We want to eliminate duplication of like fields meanwhile having but have some fields (spec. Client Legal Name) show up on all forms – can we assign a field to multiple forms? Can we make it editable only on one form and just make to viewable when it shows on the others?

8. Make the Type field a drop-down. We don't know all the options, and a dropdown would be easier.

9. We need to have the email function built in so we can send emails using multiple templates that merge data into the message before sending.

10. We need a template manager so we can make email templates using a WYSIWYG editor, and assign templates to certain forms. I need this new template manager to be secured to only certain people.

11. We will also need to have a naming convention for including data from the form in the email. I assume that we might be able to use the database name (i.e. the formname.itemname).

12. We would like to test this using the Current Info form. At the bottom, we want to make a button that says email this form using Template XXX (and display the template name that was assigned to this form in Step 2 above).

13. Finally, we need some additional security so that only certain people can use the email function. This is important because we can set certain fields to be hidden, read only, or changeable. I don't want a user with limited access being able to email the whole form out (it would defeat the purpose of hiding the fields). So, if we could control that email form button through security, I think that's ideal.

In addition, in order to create a WYSIWYG email template editor/builder for the CRM System, I integrated an Open Source Email template editor (Mosaico) into the CRM by building a custom PHP Codeigniter and Database Backend for the initially Client-side only Template Editor. This Codeigniter Integration of "Mosaico" is Open Source and Free for Commercial use. You can get it at :
https://github.com/ar27111994/Mosaico-CodeIgniter-Ion-Auth

Or

you can also check its live demo at:
http://mosaicoci.ar27111994.com/

items.title: Web RTC
items.description:
The task was to create a video conferencing web application exactly like https://appr.tc/, but with a database-backend to store the currently active and non-active rooms. For this purpose, I used Codeigniter PHP as the  web serving and database backend, whereas the Node.js app was used for real-time communication and hosted via Heroku at:
https://ar-webrtc.herokuapp.com

items.title: JMGARTS.com Page Speed Optimization
items.description: My client wanted to increase page load speed of their website. To achieve this goal, I optimized website's images, improved server caching, added expire headers for different file types, enabled lazy loading of images etc. After all this, I achieved a page speed of 91% on PageSpeed and 88% on YSlow.

items.title: https://directsoftwareoutlet.com/ Payment Gateway Integration
items.description: My client asked me to test out different payment gateways for their Australian Woocommerce Store and after some testing and consideration, we settled on PayPal Merchant Payment, which I setup on the website for them. Moreover, I also added Payment Gateway Badges in the header and footer of the installed Woocommerce theme.

items.title: Aljibouri.co
items.description: My client wanted to present a responsive thumbnail grid of some specified post categories and some specified posts on the Homepage. The client also wanted special an image colored zoom hover effect on the above-mentioned thumbnails and their headings alongside some other minor adjustments to the template. For the first task, I created custom shortcodes in the theme's function.php file. I added custom CSS3 Code in the theme's custom code area provided by default.

items.title: PDF to Responsive HTML 5
items.description: Had to Convert Non-text a PDF File to Responsive HTML5, so that it could be sent as an HTML Email with some Links in it. Therefore, to achieve this, I converted the PDF to SVG via a free online tool and embedded that image into an HTML5 file with some links wrapped around SVG objects that represented the specified parts of the PDF. I also used SVG Specific JQuery to link the rest of the non-linked document and SVG objects to the Client's website as instructed.

items.title: American Community School Donations Page
items.description: My client wanted to have a donation form integrated with Mastercard International Gateway Service with full validation checks, Google Captcha Verification all with a secure and tight integration with a database backend to store the data regarding all valid transactions.

items.title: Ionic 3 Chart and Forms with Validation and Native SQLite Storage
items.description:
My Client wanted me to develop a small app with the following features:

- form on 2 pages ( 1 form for MM/YYYY, weight, height; 1 form for satisfaction score)
- form validation ( MM/YYYY, weight, height, satisfaction required; age from MM/YYYY > 18; weight and height are numbers)
- storage set and get n data with date-time ( MM/YYYY, weight, height, satisfaction)
- base Chart with n satisfaction score set ( satisfaction score on the abscissa, the date on the ordinate)

items.title: POC for Citrix App Layering on Azure
items.description: Created a Microsoft Remote Desktop Services project with Citrix App Layering on Azure Integration, learning everything as I went along with the project. The final implementation included high availability load balancing and file server clustering ensuring a scalable, high-performance setup for the POC that could be easily replicated in a production environment with minimum changes.

items.title: Eagle 6
items.description:
Eagle 6 is a cybersecurity product that secures large enterprises by detecting unknown vulnerabilities before they expose systems to risk. My primary role included the implementation of the frontend portion of different features and modules of the product using Angular and a host of other related premium and open source libraries in an Agile-driven environment.

- Implemented Frontend for Cloud Storage and Organization Chart Modeling Modules of the flagship enterprise product of the company alongside the integration of various rich text document editing tools and their customization via angular-ized plugins.
- Implemented frontend for file and folder management including role-based permissions assignment for each file/folder, ability to preview and edit contents of each file/folder based on the type of file, and user's permission.
- Implemented complex plugin-based online editors for opening both short and full-featured documents with the ability to view/edit Microsoft Office files and support for comments, concurrent editing, permission-based content viewing/editing, and pdf export.
- Implemented frontend for chunked file and folder upload using TUS protocol for scalability and support for uploading large files.
- Implemented a new iteration of the Organization Chart Modeling tool in GOJS architecting the data access and state management of this module using Akita (Redux, Flux based State Management Library) for scalability and robust maintenance.
- Implemented and Maintained different types of graphs and maps of Network Monitoring tools module using d3, Highcharts, leaflet, and OSM Tiles server.
- Implemented different components of the app from concept through deployment.
- Interviewed and facilitated the hiring of multiple Frontend developers for the team I was assigned to.
- Facilitated in driving code quality and scalability by restructuring and implementing Redux, Flux based data flow & state management for different modules & components alongside reviewing, providing feedback to fellow UI developers on their code contributions.
- Standardized all output with a new responsive & accessible UI using Google material design standards.
- Assessed UX and UI designs for technical feasibility and provided estimates for their implementation.
- Collaborated with product team members to implement new feature developments.
- Worked in an Agile-driven environment to effectively maintain project timelines and utilize available resources.
- Stack used: Angular 2+, Angular Material, TUS, RxJS, Akita (Redux, Flux based State Management library), GoJS, D3, Highcharts, leaflet, OSM, Typescript, BEM, Jest & SCSS.
